title: Plasma membranes
description: Test your knowledge of plasma membranes!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes how phospholipid regions are positioned in the cell membrane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Hydrophilic phosphate regions are positioned toward aqueous environments.
Hydrophobic fatty acid regions are positioned toward aqueous environments.
Hydrophilic phosphate regions face each other within the interior of the cell membrane.
Hydrophobic fatty acid regions face each other within the exterior of the cell membrane.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes how regions of phospholipids are arranged in the cell membrane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Hydrophobic fatty acid regions face each other within the interior of the cell membrane.
Hydrophobic fatty acid regions are positioned toward polar environments.
Hydrophilic phosphate regions face each other within the interior of the cell membrane.
Hydrophilic phosphate regions are positioned toward nonpolar environments.

assessment_items.question_content:
An embedded membrane protein spans the entire cell membrane of a single cell. 

**Which of the following must be true about the amino acids that make up the protein?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Amino acids with charged side groups are in contact with the phosphate region of the cell membrane.
Amino acids with nonpolar side groups are in contact with the phosphate region of the cell membrane.
Amino acids with polar side groups are in contact with the fatty acid region of the cell membrane.
Amino acids with uncharged side groups are in contact with the phosphate region of the cell membrane.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true about the amino acids that make up a transmembrane protein?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Amino acids with nonpolar side groups are in contact with the fatty acid region of the cell membrane.
Amino acids with polar side groups are in contact with the fatty acid region of the cell membrane.
Amino acids with nonpolar side groups are in contact with the phosphate region of the cell membrane.
Amino acids with charged side groups are in contact with the fatty acid region of the cell membrane.

assessment_items.question_content:
The images below show the 3D space-filling models of two lipids. One lipid has a straight structure and the other lipid has a kinked, or bent structure. 


[[☃ image 1]]

*Image credit: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons*

**Which of the following best describes how kinks within lipids contribute to the fluidity of the cell membrane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The fatty acid on the left shows 8 gray circles arranged in a straight line with 2 red circles at the right end of the line. The fatty acid on the right shows 8 gray circles with a bend between the fourth and fifth circle to form an inverted v shape. On the right end of the bent structure there are 2 red circles.

choices.content: The hydrocarbon tails of unsaturated fatty acids form kinks that prevent dense packing of components within the cell membrane.
The hydrocarbon tails of saturated fatty acids form kinks that prevent dense packing of components within the cell membrane.
The double bonds within the phosphate heads of phospholipids form kinks that prevent dense packing of components within the cell membrane.
The double bonds within the glycerol portions of fatty acids form kinks that prevent dense packing of components within the cell membrane.

assessment_items.question_content:
The images below show the 3D space-filling models of two fatty acids. One fatty acid has a straight structure and the other fatty acid has a kinked, or bent structure. 


[[☃ image 1]]

*Image credit: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons*

**Which of the following correctly describes how high levels of fatty acid structures contribute to the rigidity of the cell membrane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The fatty acid on the left shows 8 gray circles arranged in a straight line with 2 red circles at the right end of the line. The fatty acid on the right shows 8 gray circles with a bend between the fourth and fifth circle to form an inverted v shape. On the right end of the bent structure there are 2 red circles.

choices.content: The cell membrane becomes more rigid as the amount of saturated fatty acids with a straight structure increases.
The cell membrane becomes less rigid as the amount of saturated fatty acids with a straight structure increases.
The cell membrane becomes more rigid as the amount of unsaturated fatty acids with a kinked structure increases.
The cell membrane becomes less rigid as the amount of unsaturated fatty acids with a straight structure increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes how high levels of cholesterol affect the cell membrane at certain temperatures?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: At low temperatures, high amounts of cholesterol increase the fluidity of the cell membrane.
At high temperatures, high amounts of cholesterol increase the fluidity of the cell membrane.
At low temperatures, high amounts of cholesterol increase the rigidity of the cell membrane.
At high temperatures, high amounts of cholesterol decrease the rigidity of the cell membrane.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes how high levels of cholesterol affect the cell membrane at certain temperatures?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: At high temperatures, high amounts of cholesterol decrease the fluidity of the cell membrane.
At low temperatures, high amounts of cholesterol decrease the fluidity of the cell membrane.
At low temperatures, high amounts of cholesterol increase the rigidity of the cell membrane.
At high temperatures, high amounts of cholesterol decrease the rigidity of the cell membrane.
